Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Whistler: Live eBPF Programming from the Common Lisp REPL 30 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. CERN to host a new phase of Open Research Europe 195 upvotes, 16 comments
  3. Order Granting Preliminary Injunction – Anthropic vs. U.S. Department of War [pdf] 102 upvotes, 10 comments
  4. OpenTelemetry profiles enters public alpha 146 upvotes, 17 comments
  5. Show HN: Fio: 3D World editor/game engine – inspired by Radiant and Hammer 39 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Why so many control rooms were seafoam green (2025) 602 upvotes, 117 comments
  7. How much precision can you squeeze out of a table? 45 upvotes, 4 comments
  8. Show HN: Turbolite – a SQLite VFS serving sub-250ms cold JOIN queries from S3 113 upvotes, 25 comments
  9. Show HN: Veil – Dark mode PDFs without destroying images, runs in the browser 39 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. Stripe Projects: Provision and manage services from the CLI 109 upvotes, 28 comments
  11. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 291 upvotes, 90 comments
  12. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 866 upvotes, 300 comments
  13. John Bradley, author of xv, has died 220 upvotes, 68 comments
  14. DOOM Over DNS 214 upvotes, 66 comments
  15. $500 GPU outperforms Claude Sonnet on coding benchmarks 73 upvotes, 21 comments
  16. Cloudflare's Gen 13 servers: trading cache for cores for 2x performance 67 upvotes, 19 comments
  17. Show HN: I put an AI agent on a $7/month VPS with IRC as its transport layer 90 upvotes, 28 comments
  18. My minute-by-minute response to the LiteLLM malware attack 298 upvotes, 124 comments
  19. HyperAgents: Self-referential self-improving agents 135 upvotes, 57 comments
  20. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 525 upvotes, 262 comments
  21. Judge blocks Pentagon effort to 'punish' Anthropic with supply chain risk label 165 upvotes, 80 comments
  22. Chicago artist creates tourism posters for city's neighborhoods 47 upvotes, 19 comments
  23. Using FireWire on a Raspberry Pi 56 upvotes, 27 comments
  24. Anthropic Subprocessor Changes 42 upvotes, 19 comments
  25. We haven't seen the worst of what gambling and prediction markets will do 566 upvotes, 403 comments
  26. Swift 6.3 291 upvotes, 199 comments
  27. Colibri – chat platform built on the AT Protocol for communities big and small 99 upvotes, 62 comments
  28. Dobase – Your workspace, your server 13 upvotes, 4 comments
  29. Apple discontinues the Mac Pro with no plans for future hardware 76 upvotes, 66 comments
  30. From 0% to 36% on Day 1 of ARC-AGI-3 5 upvotes, 4 comments

Last generated 10:00am, 27 March 2026. Source here.
